evidenceRefs: swift test: testPipelineDeliversFramesAndRussianLine — synthetic frames reached the pipeline (non-zero)
swift test: testPipelineDeliversFramesAndRussianLine + testReducerRendersRussianLine — Russian line rendered
live smoke run by the owner with their OpenAI key: real EN->RU subtitles on English YouTube and on a video call; translation quality reviewed and accepted by the owner over daily use (AT-003)
swift test: testReducerStopClearsSubtitle — stop clears subtitles
swift test: testReconnectResumesWithoutCrashing — reconnect resumes, no crash
swift test: testFileKeyStoreRoundTrip — BYOK key store/read/delete
live use by the owner: Option-Q quick-translate returns a faithful translation of typed words/phrases in both directions in well under a second (GLM-4.7 on Cerebras, reasoning off)

SMAppService.mainApp registered the app as a login item; verified present in the macOS Background Task Management database — `sfltool dumpbtm` lists com.kiln.realtimetranslator -> /Applications/RealtimeTranslator.app (may need admin to dump); relaunches at login per SMAppService (manual_audit)

loggingImplemented: os_log subsystem com.kiln.realtimetranslator: session lifecycle, connection state, reconnect attempts, error categories (never payloads)

notes: TranslatorKit core: 5/5 swift tests pass (pipeline, reconnect, start/stop, BYOK keystore) — no keys required
the app builds (swift build -c release) and passes launch-smoke as a menu-bar agent

engine sits behind the TranslationEngine protocol (fake for tests, real OpenAI Realtime WebSocket impl wired); capture behind AudioSource (ScreenCaptureKit); keys behind KeyStore (Keychain + dev file fallback)
os_log observability implemented (subsystem com.kiln.realtimetranslator) — never logs keys, audio, or transcripts
capture is system-audio only — no microphone entitlement or usage requested
quick-translate (Option-Q) and login-item (SMAppService) are implemented and verified by the owner's live use (VER-007) and the login-item status check (VER-008)

honest gate: the VER-003 quality live smoke has now been run by the owner with their key and accepted over daily use, so dev is ready_for_release — the gate held until the quality check was genuinely satisfied, not before

openRisks: the real provider event shapes and audio encoding are validated by the live smoke; a provider-side protocol change would require re-validation
the ScreenCaptureKit system-audio path needs Screen Recording permission granted at runtime
changeLog: rev1: implemented from kiln-arch rev1 — TranslatorKit core (5/5 tests) + app shell + real capture/provider/keystore behind protocols; blocked_on_environment pending the VER-003 quality live smoke (owner key)

rev2: resynced to the shipped app on kiln-arch rev2 — corrected the engine file to GPTRealtimeTranslateEngine.swift, added IMP-QUICKTRANSLATE (QuickTranslate.swift) and IMP-LOGINITEM (SMAppService); the owner ran the VER-003 live smoke and accepted quality, so status -> ready_for_release; re-pinned spec rev3 + arch rev2
